title: Melandrium requienii
taxonomy: Melandrium requienii is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is also known as Red Catchfly. The genus Melandrium is a group of annual or biennial plants native to Europe and Asia. Melandrium requienii is a type of flowering plant that is commonly found in dry, sandy soils and is often used in landscaping and gardening. Melandrium requienii is a member of the order Caryophyllales.
uses: Melandrium requienii, also known as Alpine Catchfly, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
